explanation: This command attempts to load the specified DLL using PowerShell. It mirrors the Sigma rule's detection criteria for 'Import-Module' and the DLL name.
This command uses the shorthand 'ipmo' for 'Import-Module' and targets pwsh.exe, which is also covered by the Sigma rule.
This command uses single quotes around the DLL name, a safe mutation technique that should still be detected by the Sigma rule.
This command uses an encoded space character (\x20) between the cmdlet and the DLL name, a safe mutation technique that may bypass detection if the rule does not account for such encoding.
This command uses URL-encoded space (\x20) to separate the cmdlet and the DLL name, a safe mutation technique that may bypass detection if the rule does not account for such encoding.
This command uses a relative path to the DLL, a safe mutation technique that may bypass detection if the rule does not account for path variations.
This command uses an absolute path to the DLL, a safe mutation technique that may bypass detection if the rule does not account for path variations.
This command adds the '-Force' flag, which is optional but does not change the core detection logic of the Sigma rule.
This command uses the full parameter name '-Name' instead of the implicit parameter, a safe mutation technique that should still be detected by the Sigma rule.
This command appends a benign command after the import, a safe mutation technique that may bypass detection if the rule does not account for additional commands.
